name: Sa'd ibn al-Harith
title: The Repentant
shortDescription: A brother from the Khawarij of Kufa who was initially in Umar Sa'd's army but repented upon hearing Imam Hussain's (AS) call for help and was martyred.
fullStory:
Sa'd ibn al-Harith al-Ansari (al-Ajlani) and his brother 'Abu al-Hutuf' were residents of Kufa and belonged to the Khawarij of Nahrawan. They harbored an old grudge against Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and thus eagerly joined Umar Sa'd's army in Karbala to fight the son of Ali (AS).

They were present on the front of falsehood throughout the day of Ashura, witnessing the martyrdom of the companions and Bani Hashem. On the afternoon of Ashura, when all of Imam Hussain's (AS) companions were martyred, the Imam stood alone against thousands and called out for help: 'Is there any helper to help me? Is there any defender to defend the sanctuary of the Messenger of God?'

This lonely voice was answered with the crying and wailing of the women and children of the Ahl al-Bayt from the tents. Hearing the cries of the Prophet's (PBUH) daughters sent such a tremor through Sa'd and his brother that the veils of ignorance were suddenly lifted from their eyes. They remembered the Prophet's (PBUH) words about Imam Hussain (AS).

Sa'd turned to his brother and said: 'We say 'there is no judgment but God's', but this Hussain is the son of our Prophet's daughter, through whose grandfather we hope for intercession on the Day of Judgment; how can we leave him alone among these oppressors?' Zeal and the light of guidance flared in the hearts of these two brothers, and they changed their path at the most critical moment in history.

They drew their swords and, with the Khawarij slogan ('There is no judgment but God's') but this time against the army of disbelief, attacked Umar Sa'd's forces. Weeping, Sa'd and his brother killed and wounded a number of enemies until they were finally martyred just steps away from the Master of Martyrs (AS), achieving a blessed end.
quoteArabic: أَلا ناصِرٌ يَنْصُرُني؟ أَلا ذابٌّ يَذُبُّ عَنْ حَرَمِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ؟
quote: (Transformed upon hearing the Imam's call:) Is there any helper to help me? Is there any defender to defend the Prophet's sanctuary?
